# BUG-001 Writeup
> Generated by [Quality Playbook](https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook) v1.4.6 — Andrew Stellman
> Date: 2026-04-20 · Project: virtio-1.4.6

## 1. Summary

REQ-002 requires reset to remain transport-visibly incomplete until status reads back `0`, but `vm_reset()` in `drivers/virtio/virtio_mmio.c:251-257` only writes `0` and returns.

## 2. Why this is a bug

The MMIO transport exposes reset as complete before the formal reset contract says later reinitialization is safe. PCI modern already implements the required read-back poll, so the MMIO omission creates a real cross-transport reset-completion mismatch.

## 3. Regression test

- **Requirement:** REQ-002
- **Test:** `quality/test_regression.py::TestCodeReviewRegressions.test_bug_001_mmio_reset_waits_for_status_clear`
- **Regression patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-001-regression-test.patch`

## 4. FAIL -> PASS evidence

```text
RED: Command: python3 <direct regression assertion runner> test_bug_001_mmio_reset_waits_for_status_clear
RED: AssertionError: Regex didn't match: 'readl\\(vm_dev->base \\+ VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS\\)'
GREEN: Command: python3 <temp source patcher BUG-001> && python3 <direct regression assertion runner> test_bug_001_mmio_reset_waits_for_status_clear
GREEN: test_bug_001_mmio_reset_waits_for_status_clear: PASS
```

See `quality/results/BUG-001.red.log` and `quality/results/BUG-001.green.log`.

## 5. Challenge gate

`quality/challenge/BUG-001-challenge.md` -> **CONFIRMED**

## 6. The fix

- **Fix patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-001-fix.patch`

```diff
diff --git a/drivers/virtio/virtio_mmio.c b/drivers/virtio/virtio_mmio.c
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/drivers/virtio/virtio_mmio.c
+++ b/drivers/virtio/virtio_mmio.c
@@ -251,7 +251,11 @@ static void vm_reset(struct virtio_device *vdev)
 {
 	struct virtio_mmio_device *vm_dev = to_virtio_mmio_device(vdev);
 
 	/* 0 status means a reset. */
 	writel(0, vm_dev->base + VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS);
+	while (readl(vm_dev->base + VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) & 0xff)
+		cpu_relax();
 }
```
